title: fritillaria lusitanica subsp. stenophylla
intro: Fritillaria lusitanica subsp. stenophylla, also known as the narrow-leaved Fritillaria, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the lily family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been employed to treat a variety of health conditions, such as respiratory issues, coughs, and sore throats. The health benefits of Fritillaria lusitanica subsp. stenophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate congestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of the plant are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have therapeutic properties and are commonly used in traditional medicine.
